title: Sparta Announce New Album Cut A Silhouette: Hear The Frank Iero Collab “Crater”
author: Danielle Chelosky

The post Sparta Announce New Album <em>Cut A Silhouette</em>: Hear The Frank Iero Collab “Crater” appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Sparta are back. The post-hardcore crew's last LP was 2022's self-titled, and today they're announcing its followup titled Cut A Silhouette. The lead single "Crater" is a ripper, and it was co-written with My Chemical Romance's Frank Iero.

"This is a love record," frontman Jim Ward says. "I want it to leave an impression, and at this point in my career, that's what I yearn for. I'm working so hard to make something meaningful to me, and I really want people to get a chance to feel it as well."

Cut A Silhouette was produced, engineered, and recorded in seven days at Magpie Cage Recording Studio by Jawbox's J. Robbins. The track "Mouthbreather" was also co-written with Iero, and other collaborators on the album include Chevelle's Kemble Walters, Brooks Harlan of Jawbox and War On Women, Carlos Arévalo of Chicano Batman, and Adrian Bourgeois. Watch the Travis Shinn-directed music video for "Crater" below.

TRACKLIST:
01 "Split Lip"
02 "Crater"
03 "Mouthbreather"
04 "Daydream"
05 "See You Soon"
06 "Everything You Say"
07 "Without Your Hands"
08 "Midnights"
09 "Mystery Of Missing"
10 "Glimmer"

Cut A Silhouette is out 5/29 via Equal Vision / Dine Alone. Pre-order it here.

John Carluccio
